zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Sinclair of Deskford於1347年出生在英國蘇格蘭班福爾德斯福德時，他的父親William Sinclair 8th Lord of Roslin19歲，母親Isabella Strathearn19歲。他與Elizabeth Douglas結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1411年7月24日在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾哈拉福享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Sinclair of Deskford于1347年出生在英国苏格兰班福爾德斯福德时，他的父亲William Sinclair 8th Lord of Roslin19岁，母亲Isabella Strathearn19岁。他与Elizabeth Douglas结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1411年7月24日在英国苏格兰阿爾迪史爾哈拉福享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. en.lifeSummary: When Richard Sinclair of Deskford was born in 1347, in Deskford, Banffshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, William Sinclair 8th Lord of Roslin, was 19 and his mother, Isabella Strathearn, was 19. He married Elizabeth Douglas. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 24 July 1411, in Harlaw, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Sinclair of Deskford est né en 1347, à Deskford, Banffshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Sinclair 8th Lord of Roslin, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Isabella Strathearn, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Douglas. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 24 juillet 1411, à Harlaw, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans.

Er hat Elizabeth Douglas geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 24. Juli 1411 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Harlaw, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Sinclair of Deskford è nato il nel 1347 nel Deskford, Banffshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William Sinclair 8th Lord of Roslin, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Isabella Strathearn, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Douglas. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 24 luglio 1411, nel Harlaw, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Sinclair of Deskford(이)가 1347년 영국 스코틀랜드 반프실르 데스k퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Sinclair 8th Lord of Roslin(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella Strathearn(은)는 19세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Douglas(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1411년 7월 24일 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 하를로에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
